engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor decides by browsing photos, the menu, promos, locations and hours of a physical restaurant — checking a PLACE is the whole page job. No defined expertise sold, no portfolio-as-proof, offer not pre-understood.

goal.one_action: Reserve a table (or place an order) at a Flying Sushi location

structure.archetype_note: Multi-page restaurant-chain site: home + menu + promotions + per-location profile pages, with a secondary B2B franchise-inquiry lead form and all transactional CTAs (reserve/order) handed off to third-party tools.

mapped.observed_as: top nav (Início, Cardápio, Promoções, Reserva, Faça seu pedido, Seja um franqueado...)
Todo o sabor que você gosta está aqui!
Flying Sushi: amor, sabor e dedicação (25+ years about)
Faça a sua reserva! / Faça seu pedido

unmapped.observed: Promotions (kids free, TPM women's Tuesday, birthday coupon MEUNIVERNOFLYING, Valentine's) with prices & coupon codes
unmapped.why_no_fit: Recurring restaurant promo/offer blocks with coupons and exact prices are not features/pricing/useCases in the landing-page vocab

unmapped.observed: Encontre o Flying Sushi perto de você! — grid of 6 locations with address/hours/phone
unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical 'locations' section; this is a branch directory, not testimonials/socialProof

unmapped.observed: Cardápio — course-grouped dish lists (Entrada, Temakis, Sashimis...) with no prices
unmapped.why_no_fit: A restaurant menu is the defining place artifact; no canonical menu section exists and it is not pricing (no prices) nor features

unmapped.observed: Butantã single-location page (address, hours, phone, map)
unmapped.why_no_fit: Per-branch detail/profile page, not a landing section

proposed_new.name: practitioner-profiles
proposed_new.evidence: 6 near-identical /unidades/<bairro>/ per-location profile subpages (each with own address/hours/phone/map) for a multi-branch business — the per-location variant of the registered practitioner-profiles capability.

proposed_new.name: menu-display
proposed_new.evidence: Cardápio page: course-grouped dish lists (Entrada, Pratos quentes, Temakis, Hossomakis, Uramakis, Niguiri, Sashimis) with no prices and no cart — a restaurant menu, the core place artifact, which the closed vocab (catalog implies products/cart) cannot express.

design_style.none_fits_because: Capture recorded no fonts, colors, or dark/light; only 'photo-heavy'. Insufficient signal to assign one of the six styles.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is 'rendered' not 'full' (franquia form only confirmed via rendered fallback; only 1 of 6 location pages sampled) — capped at medium per rules. Engine and structure are nonetheless clear.

gap_findings: No canonical section for a restaurant MENU (course-grouped dish list, no prices, no cart) — closest closed capability 'catalog' implies purchasable products.
No canonical section for restaurant PROMOTIONS/OFFERS (coupon codes, day-of-week deals, birthday/kids pricing) — a defining place-engine content type.
No canonical section for a LOCATIONS/branch directory nor operating-hours; map-hours capability covers the data but there is no section id to place it.

Place-engine restaurants routinely fan multiple transactional CTAs (reserve / order / WhatsApp) out to different third-party tools — the single 'one_action' model flattens a genuinely multi-CTA intent.
